finding-001.description: MCP tools follow a consistent registration pattern via registerAllTools() in registry.ts. Each tool is defined in a separate file with registerXxx(server) function using ArkType for parameter validation.
finding-001.recommendation: Auth tools should follow same pattern: auth.register, auth.login, auth.refresh, auth.logout in separate files, registered via registerAuthTools() added to registry.ts

finding-002.description: HTTP transport in server-http.ts uses FastMCP with stateful session tracking via mcp-session-id header. Server supports both SSE streaming and request-response patterns.
finding-002.recommendation: JWT auth middleware can intercept before FastMCP processing. Check Authorization header for 'Bearer <token>' on protected endpoints.

finding-003.description: Web app uses React Context for state injection. WavesmithStoreContext.tsx shows pattern: createContext, Provider component with useRef for stable instance, useWavesmithStore hook with error handling.
finding-003.relevantCode: const StoreContext = createContext<any>(null); export function WavesmithStoreProvider({ schema, children })
finding-003.recommendation: Create AuthContext.tsx following same pattern: AuthProvider with user state, useAuth hook. Store JWT in memory (not localStorage for XSS protection), refresh token in httpOnly cookie ideally.

finding-004.description: MCPService class in mcpService.ts handles all HTTP communication with MCP server. Manages session initialization, tool calls, and SSE streaming. Uses fetch with custom headers.
finding-004.recommendation: Add Authorization header with JWT token. Create method setAuthToken(token) to store token and include in all requests. Token should be passed from AuthContext after login.

finding-005.description: No password hashing library exists in the codebase. Requirements specify bcrypt or argon2 with secure defaults (req-auth-006).
finding-005.recommendation: Add 'bcrypt' or '@node-rs/argon2' to MCP package dependencies. Argon2 is recommended as it's the winner of the Password Hashing Competition and more resistant to GPU attacks.

finding-006.description: No JWT library exists in the codebase. Need library for signing/verifying tokens with support for RS256 or HS256.
finding-006.recommendation: Add 'jose' package - modern, zero-dependency JWT library that works in all JS runtimes including Bun. Supports all standard algorithms.

finding-007.description: App routing uses react-router-dom v7 with BrowserRouter in App.tsx. Routes are defined declaratively with Route components. Navigation component shows active state styling.
finding-007.recommendation: Create ProtectedRoute component wrapper that checks auth state from AuthContext and redirects to /login if unauthenticated. Add /login and /register routes.

finding-008.description: FileSystemPersistence stores entities as JSON files. User passwords will be stored hashed in .schemas/auth-layer/data/User.json. This is acceptable for MVP but production should use encrypted storage.
finding-008.recommendation: Current persistence is adequate for MVP. passwordHash field already designed in schema. Consider adding data encryption at rest for production (future enhancement).

finding-009.description: MCP tool tests use bun:test with beforeEach reset pattern. Tests directly call tool handler logic extracted from registration. Pattern: define handleXxx function, test it directly, then register it.
finding-009.recommendation: Auth tool tests should follow same pattern. Test auth.register, auth.login, auth.refresh, auth.logout handlers with mock users. Test JWT expiration, invalid credentials, token refresh scenarios.

finding-010.description: FastMCP httpStream transport doesn't expose native middleware hooks. JWT validation may need to happen at tool level rather than transport level, or require custom HTTP wrapper.
finding-010.recommendation: Two options: (1) Validate JWT inside each protected tool handler, or (2) Wrap FastMCP's HTTP handler with custom Express/Bun middleware. Option 1 is simpler but requires per-tool boilerplate. Option 2 is cleaner but adds complexity.

finding-011.description: Hooks in apps/web/src/hooks/ follow React hooks convention. useAgentChat demonstrates state management with useState, useCallback, useRef pattern for async operations with session tracking.
finding-011.relevantCode: const sessionIdRef = useRef<string | null>(null); sessionIdRef.current = state.sessionId
finding-011.recommendation: Create useAuth hook that exposes: user, isAuthenticated, isLoading, login(), logout(), register(). Use similar ref pattern for token to avoid stale closure issues.

finding-012.description: Auth-layer schema already exists with User and RefreshToken entities. User has id, email, passwordHash, timestamps. RefreshToken has user reference, token (hashed), expiresAt, revokedAt for logout support.
finding-012.relevantCode: User: { id, email, passwordHash, createdAt, updatedAt }, RefreshToken: { id, user (ref), token, expiresAt, revokedAt, createdAt, userAgent }
finding-012.recommendation: Schema is complete and matches requirements. Load with schema.load('auth-layer') before auth tools use store.create/store.list operations.
